expectations.text: CollectionView displays messages from an ObservableCollection
expectations.evidence: ChatViewModel.cs line 15 declares `public ObservableCollection<Message> Messages { get; } = new();`. ChatPage.xaml line 85 binds `ItemsSource="{Binding Messages}"` on the CollectionView named `MessagesCollectionView`. This is a genuine binding to a live ObservableCollection, not a stub.

expectations.text: Message alignment differs by sender — right-aligned for current user, left-aligned for others
expectations.evidence: MessageTemplateSelector.cs returns `MyMessageTemplate` when `IsFromMe == true` and `TheirMessageTemplate` otherwise. In ChatPage.xaml: MyMessageTemplate places the Border in `Grid.Column="1"` with a `Width="*"` spacer column on the left (lines 18-38), pushing content right. TheirMessageTemplate places the Border in `Grid.Column="0"` with the spacer on the right (lines 42-70), pushing content left. The selector is wired via `ItemTemplate="{StaticResource MessageTemplateSelector}"`.

expectations.text: Code calls CollectionView.ScrollTo() to scroll to the last message when new messages arrive
expectations.evidence: ChatPage.xaml.cs lines 30-33: `MessagesCollectionView.ScrollTo(item: message, position: ScrollToPosition.End, animate: true);` is called in the `ScrollToBottom(Message message)` method. This method is invoked as a callback registered with the ViewModel, which fires it whenever the CollectionChanged event fires for new items.

expectations.text: A CollectionChanged or property-change handler triggers the scroll behavior
expectations.evidence: ChatViewModel.cs line 39: `Messages.CollectionChanged += OnMessagesCollectionChanged;`. The handler `OnMessagesCollectionChanged` (lines 74-85) checks for `NotifyCollectionChangedAction.Add`, extracts the new message, and calls `_scrollToBottomCallback?.Invoke(newMessage)` on the main thread. This directly ties every new item added to the scroll action.

expectations.text: Message model has properties for content/text, a sender or IsFromMe flag, and optionally timestamp
expectations.evidence: Message.cs defines: `public string Text { get; set; }` (content), `public bool IsFromMe { get; set; }` (sender/direction flag), `public string SenderName { get; set; }` (sender identity), and `public DateTime Timestamp { get; set; }` (optional timestamp). All required properties are present.

expectations.text: CollectionView has IsGrouped='True'
expectations.evidence: ProductCatalogPage.xaml line 14: <CollectionView ItemsSource="{Binding ProductGroups}" IsGrouped="True">

expectations.text: GroupHeaderTemplate shows category name and item count
expectations.evidence: ProductCatalogPage.xaml lines 17-47: GroupHeaderTemplate DataTemplate binds CategoryName (line 28: Text="{Binding CategoryName}") and ItemCount with StringFormat (line 41: Text="{Binding ItemCount, StringFormat='{0} items'}"). ProductGroup.cs exposes both properties: CategoryName (string) and ItemCount (int, computed as Count).

expectations.text: Price is formatted as currency (StringFormat with C, or similar currency formatting)
expectations.evidence: ProductCatalogPage.xaml line 87: Text="{Binding Price, StringFormat='{0:C}'}" — uses the standard .NET currency format specifier C which formats as locale currency.

expectations.text: An 'Add to Cart' button has a Command binding
expectations.evidence: ProductCatalogPage.xaml lines 74-83: Button with Text="Add to Cart" and Command="{Binding Source={RelativeSource AncestorType={x:Type viewmodels:ProductCatalogViewModel}}, Path=AddToCartCommand}" and CommandParameter="{Binding}". ProductCatalogViewModel.cs line 38: AddToCartCommand = new Command<Product>(OnAddToCart).

expectations.text: Pull-to-refresh is implemented via RefreshView or IsBusy pattern
expectations.evidence: ProductCatalogPage.xaml lines 10-11: <RefreshView IsRefreshing="{Binding IsRefreshing}" Command="{Binding RefreshCommand}"> wrapping the CollectionView. ViewModel has IsRefreshing bool property (lines 22-30) and RefreshCommand (line 37) that calls LoadCatalogAsync and resets IsRefreshing in a finally block (line 68).

expectations.text: CollectionView has an EmptyView defined for the no-results state
expectations.evidence: ProductCatalogPage.xaml lines 97-114: <CollectionView.EmptyView> containing a <ContentView> wrapping a VerticalStackLayout with Labels: 'No products found' (FontSize=18, Bold) and 'Pull down to refresh the catalog.'

expectations.text: ViewModel uses a grouped collection structure (ObservableCollection of group objects)
expectations.evidence: ProductCatalogViewModel.cs lines 9 and 12-19: private ObservableCollection<ProductGroup> _productGroups and public ObservableCollection<ProductGroup> ProductGroups property. ProductGroup inherits List<Product> (ProductGroup.cs line 3), satisfying MAUI's grouping requirement. LoadCatalogAsync groups products using LINQ GroupBy and wraps results in new ObservableCollection<ProductGroup>.

expectations.text: CollectionView uses a 2-column GridItemsLayout (Span=2 or Columns=2)
expectations.evidence: RecipesPage.xaml lines 16-21: '<GridItemsLayout Orientation="Vertical" Span="2" VerticalItemSpacing="12" HorizontalItemSpacing="12" />'

expectations.text: DataTemplate includes an Image element bound to a photo or image source property
expectations.evidence: RecipesPage.xaml lines 45-52: '<Image Grid.Row="0" Source="{Binding PhotoUrl}" Aspect="AspectFill" HeightRequest="160">' — binds to PhotoUrl property on Recipe model

expectations.text: DataTemplate includes a Label bound to recipe Name and a Label bound to prep time
expectations.evidence: RecipesPage.xaml line 72: '<Label Grid.Row="1" Text="{Binding Name}" .../>' and line 80: '<Label Grid.Row="2" Text="{Binding PrepTime, StringFormat=\'Prep: {0}\'}" .../>'

expectations.text: CollectionView has SelectionMode set to Single
expectations.evidence: RecipesPage.xaml line 11: 'SelectionMode="Single"' on the CollectionView element

expectations.text: ViewModel has a SelectedRecipe or SelectedItem property of the Recipe type with INotifyPropertyChanged
expectations.evidence: RecipesViewModel.cs lines 23-34: 'public Recipe? SelectedRecipe { get => _selectedRecipe; set { if (_selectedRecipe != value) { _selectedRecipe = value; OnPropertyChanged(); } } }' — typed as Recipe?, calls OnPropertyChanged(), class inherits BindableObject which provides INotifyPropertyChanged

expectations.text: x:DataType compiled bindings are used on the page and/or DataTemplate
expectations.evidence: RecipesPage.xaml line 7: 'x:DataType="viewmodels:RecipesViewModel"' on ContentPage, and line 24: 'x:DataType="models:Recipe"' on DataTemplate — compiled bindings applied at both levels

expectations.text: ViewModel uses ObservableCollection<Recipe> for the items source
expectations.evidence: RecipesViewModel.cs line 11: 'public ObservableCollection<Recipe> Recipes { get; } = new ObservableCollection<Recipe> { ... }' with 8 seeded items

expectations.text: CollectionView displays messages from an ObservableCollection
expectations.evidence: ChatViewModel.cs line 14 declares 'public ObservableCollection<Message> Messages { get; } = new();'. ChatPage.xaml line 115 binds the CollectionView with 'ItemsSource="{Binding Messages}"'. The connection is genuine and complete.

expectations.text: Message alignment differs by sender — right-aligned for current user, left-aligned for others
expectations.evidence: ChatPage.xaml uses two DataTriggers on a ContentView. When IsMe=true the Grid places a transparent BoxView in Column 0 and the Frame in Column 1 (pushing the bubble right). When IsMe=false the Frame is in Column 0 and the spacer is in Column 1 (keeping the bubble left). This is implemented directly in the XAML at lines 126-198.

expectations.text: Code calls CollectionView.ScrollTo() to scroll to the last message when new messages arrive
expectations.evidence: ChatPage.xaml.cs line 74: 'MessagesCollectionView.ScrollTo(lastMessage, position: ScrollToPosition.End, animate: true);' is called inside ScrollToBottom(). This method is invoked from both the CollectionChanged handler (line 60) and the ViewModel scroll delegate (line 31).

expectations.text: A CollectionChanged or property-change handler triggers the scroll behavior
expectations.evidence: ChatPage.xaml.cs lines 35 and 53-61: '_viewModel.Messages.CollectionChanged += OnMessagesCollectionChanged;' subscribes in OnBindingContextChanged. OnMessagesCollectionChanged checks for NotifyCollectionChangedAction.Add and calls ScrollToBottom(). Additionally, the ViewModel calls _scrollToBottomAction?.Invoke() directly after Messages.Add() (ChatViewModel.cs lines 63-67 and 85-86).

expectations.text: Message model has properties for content/text, a sender or IsFromMe flag, and optionally timestamp
expectations.evidence: Message.cs contains: 'public string Text { get; set; }' (content), 'public string SenderName { get; set; }' (sender), 'public bool IsMe { get; set; }' (IsFromMe flag), and 'public DateTime Timestamp { get; set; }' (timestamp). All required properties are present.

notes: Frame is deprecated in .NET MAUI; should be replaced with Border + StrokeShape='RoundRectangle 16'
DataTrigger template switching creates two visual trees per cell; DataTemplateSelector is the idiomatic and more performant pattern
The 100 ms initial scroll delay is a heuristic workaround — a SizeChanged-based approach would be more robust
DataTrigger used instead of DataTemplateSelector to keep all layout in XAML
100 ms Dispatcher.DispatchDelayed used in OnAppearing to work around layout-timing issues for initial scroll

expectations.text: CollectionView has IsGrouped='True'
expectations.evidence: ProductCatalogPage.xaml line 34: `IsGrouped="True"` is set directly on the CollectionView element. Confirmed in both the XAML output file and described in the transcript Step 3: 'IsGrouped="True" — enables group header rendering.'

expectations.text: GroupHeaderTemplate shows category name and item count
expectations.evidence: ProductCatalogPage.xaml lines 41-78: GroupHeaderTemplate DataTemplate contains a Label bound to `CategoryName` (line 54) and a Border with a Label bound to `ItemCount` with StringFormat '{0} items' (line 70). ProductGroup.cs confirms both properties: `CategoryName` (string) and `ItemCount => Count` (computed int).

expectations.text: Price is formatted as currency (StringFormat with C, or similar currency formatting)
expectations.evidence: ProductCatalogPage.xaml line 105: `Text="{Binding Price, StringFormat='{0:C}'}"` on the Price Label. The transcript explicitly notes: 'StringFormat='\'{0:C}\'' on the Price label uses the device's current culture to format the decimal as local currency (e.g., "$89.99" in en-US).'

expectations.text: An 'Add to Cart' button has a Command binding
expectations.evidence: ProductCatalogPage.xaml lines 112-122: Button with `Text="Add to Cart"` has `Command="{Binding Source={RelativeSource AncestorType={x:Type vm:ProductCatalogViewModel}}, Path=AddToCartCommand}"` and `CommandParameter="{Binding .}"`. The ViewModel exposes `public ICommand AddToCartCommand { get; }` initialized as `new Command<Product>(OnAddToCart)` (ProductCatalogViewModel.cs line 37).

expectations.text: Pull-to-refresh is implemented via RefreshView or IsBusy pattern
expectations.evidence: ProductCatalogPage.xaml lines 27-28: `<RefreshView Command="{Binding RefreshCommand}" IsRefreshing="{Binding IsRefreshing}" ...>` wraps the CollectionView. ViewModel exposes `IsRefreshing` bool property and `RefreshCommand` (ICommand). The transcript notes: 'RefreshView must be the direct parent of the scrollable view (CollectionView) to intercept the pull gesture.'

expectations.text: CollectionView has an EmptyView defined for the no-results state
expectations.evidence: ProductCatalogPage.xaml lines 131-159: A VerticalStackLayout with `IsVisible="{Binding IsEmpty}"` is overlaid in the same Grid. It contains a 'No Products Found' heading label, a descriptive subtext label, and a 'Refresh Now' button. The ViewModel sets `IsEmpty = ProductGroups.Count == 0` after loading. Note: this uses a Grid overlay pattern rather than CollectionView's built-in `EmptyView` property, which is a valid but non-standard approach.

expectations.text: ViewModel uses a grouped collection structure (ObservableCollection of group objects)
expectations.evidence: ProductCatalogViewModel.cs line 13: `public ObservableCollection<ProductGroup> ProductGroups` is the bound collection. ProductGroup.cs line 6: `public class ProductGroup : ObservableCollection<Product>` — each group extends ObservableCollection<Product>, making the overall structure an ObservableCollection of ProductGroup objects. The ViewModel's LoadCatalogAsync builds these via LINQ GroupBy (lines 54-57).

notes: BoolToObjectConverter misuse for IsVisible inversion will cause runtime binding failure — RefreshView visibility toggling will not work correctly at runtime
EmptyView implemented as a Grid overlay with IsVisible binding rather than CollectionView.EmptyView property
BoolToObjectConverter used as InvertedBoolConverter but returns strings instead of booleans — acknowledged as a known issue requiring MAUI Community Toolkit InvertedBoolConverter in production

expectations.text: CollectionView uses a 2-column GridItemsLayout (Span=2 or Columns=2)
expectations.evidence: RecipesPage.xaml lines 49-55: '<CollectionView.ItemsLayout><GridItemsLayout Orientation="Vertical" Span="2" HorizontalItemSpacing="4" VerticalItemSpacing="4" /></CollectionView.ItemsLayout>'. Span=2 is explicitly set.

expectations.text: DataTemplate includes an Image element bound to a photo or image source property
expectations.evidence: RecipesPage.xaml lines 85-89: '<Image Grid.Row="0" Aspect="AspectFill" HeightRequest="160" Source="{Binding PhotoUrl}" />'. The Image is inside the DataTemplate and bound to the PhotoUrl property on Recipe.

expectations.text: DataTemplate includes a Label bound to recipe Name and a Label bound to prep time
expectations.evidence: RecipesPage.xaml lines 92-99 show '<Label ... Text="{Binding Name}" />' and lines 109-113 show '<Label FontSize="12" Text="{Binding PrepTimeDisplay}" ... />'. Both are inside the DataTemplate. PrepTimeDisplay is a computed property on Recipe that formats PrepTimeMinutes into a display string.

expectations.text: CollectionView has SelectionMode set to Single
expectations.evidence: RecipesPage.xaml line 47 explicitly sets 'SelectionMode="None"'. The executor made a deliberate design choice to implement selection via TapGestureRecognizer + Command<Recipe> toggle instead of using the built-in CollectionView selection. The transcript Step 2 notes: 'Used CollectionView with SelectionMode="None" and a manual TapGestureRecognizer + command to keep full control over the toggle-selection behaviour.' The expectation requires SelectionMode=Single, which is absent.

expectations.text: ViewModel has a SelectedRecipe or SelectedItem property of the Recipe type with INotifyPropertyChanged
expectations.evidence: RecipesViewModel.cs lines 13-29: the class implements INotifyPropertyChanged, and lines 19-29 define 'public Recipe? SelectedRecipe { get => _selectedRecipe; set { if (_selectedRecipe == value) return; _selectedRecipe = value; OnPropertyChanged(); } }'. The property is of type Recipe?, raises PropertyChanged via OnPropertyChanged(), and the class has the full INotifyPropertyChanged event and handler.

expectations.text: x:DataType compiled bindings are used on the page and/or DataTemplate
expectations.evidence: RecipesPage.xaml line 10: 'x:DataType="vm:RecipesViewModel"' on the ContentPage element. Line 58: 'x:DataType="models:Recipe"' on the DataTemplate element. Both the page-level and DataTemplate-level compiled bindings are present.

expectations.text: ViewModel uses ObservableCollection<Recipe> for the items source
expectations.evidence: RecipesViewModel.cs line 17: 'public ObservableCollection<Recipe> Recipes { get; } = new();'. The property is of type ObservableCollection<Recipe>, initialized inline, and populated in LoadRecipes() with 10 sample Recipe objects.

notes: RelativeSource AncestorType binding to the ViewModel type inside a DataTemplate may not resolve correctly at runtime in all MAUI versions — the executor acknowledged this and suggested binding to the page as a more robust alternative.
Remote picsum.photos image URLs require internet access at runtime and would not work in an offline or production scenario.

SelectionMode is None, not Single — this is a deliberate deviation from what the expectation requires. The toggle-deselect behavior is a functional difference that may or may not be acceptable depending on the task intent.
IsSelectedConverter return type was corrected from bool to Color during design (caught before writing); this represents a real design pitfall the executor navigated without skill guidance.
